author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice
book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice.
The Canons of the CCXVII Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage. Commonly Called The Code of Canons of the African Church.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice

title: Canon XCVII.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: That there be sought from the Emperor the protection of Advocates in causes ecclesiastical.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It seemed good that the legates who were about leaving, viz., Vincent and Fortunatian, should in the name of all the provinces ask from the most glorious Emperors to give a faculty for the establishment of scholastic defensors, whose shall be the care of this very kind of business: so that as the priests of the province, they who have received the faculty as defensors of the Churches in ecclesiastical affairs, as often as necessity arises, may be able to enter the private apartments of the judges, so as to resist what is urged on the other side, or to make necessary explanations.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: That there be asked of the Emperor the appointment of Patrons for ecclesiastical heads, whose care it should be to defend the Church in its affairs, and who as priests could easily refer what things were urgent.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It seemed good that the chosen legates should have at the meeting freedom of action (legationem liberam).

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The protest of the Mauritanian bishops against Primosus.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It is evident that those of Mauritania Cæsariensis gave evidence in their own writings that Primosus had been summoned by the chiefs of the Thiganensian city, that he should present himself to the plenary council according to the imperial constitutions, and, when sought for, as was right, Primosus was not found, at least so the deacons reported.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: But since the same Mauritanians petitioned that letters be sent from the whole synod to the venerable brother, the aged Innocent, it seemed good that they should be sent, that he might know that Primosus had been sought at the council and not found at all.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The contents of this canon being special are useless, therefore no explanation has been given.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: This Canon is Canon iij. of Carthage, a.d.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: These officers [i.e. “defensors”] seem to be called “executores” in the acts of synod just before this canon.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The “priest of the province” was one chosen out of the body of advocates to be counsel to the province, to act and plead in their behalf; and that he might do it more effectually he was allowed to have private conference with the judge.
